name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
description: Orbiting the Moon since 2009 at 50 km mean lunar altitude, LRO's narrow-angle camera images at 50 cm/pixel — high enough to see Apollo descent stages, rover tracks, and astronaut footpaths still preserved in the regolith. It found Lunokhod 1 thirty-nine years after the rover went dark. It identified 200+ candidate landing sites for Artemis. The water ice deposits at the lunar south pole that drive every modern lunar programme were quantified by LRO instruments. A scout mission that became the foundation of the modern lunar era.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas V 401 from Cape Canaveral SLC-41 (carried LCROSS impactor as secondary).

events.label: EXTENDED SCIENCE
events.note: Mission extended to multi-year science phase under NASA SMD.
